title: Rezepte, Segen und Zaubersprüche aus zwei Stockholmer Handschriften
summary: The repository XML is a local copy/version of University of Michigan CME item CME00099, a keyed digital encoding of Ferdinand Holthausen's 1897 Anglia edition. Holthausen's Section I transcribes selected passages from Kungliga biblioteket X 90; Section II transcribes three charms from a separate 1597 Stockholm manuscript then designated Miscellany VII. George Stephens's 1844 edition is a prior edition that Holthausen consulted and re-collated against the manuscripts, not the sole direct source.
review_notes: The lineage is modeled as a graph because the two sections derive from different manuscript witnesses.
Access and rights statements apply only to the entity or component named in each record; they must not be inherited automatically by manuscript images, digital encodings, or project outputs.
Negative availability findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-11 and do not prove that no private or unindexed reproduction exists.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00099
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow. It is supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The XML contains IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID values of CME00099.
The source submodule is a distinct repository; do not edit this artifact while updating the lineage manifest.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00099
entities.description: A publicly accessible keyed and structurally marked-up digital text of Holthausen's 1897 article.
notes: The current item page exposes a full web text; an item-specific raw XML download URL was not verified for this record.

entities.label: Holthausen 1897 edition

bibliographic.citation: Ferdinand Holthausen, ‘Rezepte, Segen und Zaubersprüche aus zwei Stockholmer Handschriften,’ Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 19 (N.F. 7) (1897): 75–88.
bibliographic.title: Rezepte, Segen und Zaubersprüche aus zwei Stockholmer Handschriften

notes: Section I occupies printed pages 75–86 and contains items I.1–28 plus notes.
Section II begins on printed page 86 and contains three charms on pages 87–88 plus notes and a postscript.

entities.label: Kungliga biblioteket X 90
entities.description: The principal manuscript witness for Holthausen's Section I. Its manuscript leaves use page numbering, so this record deliberately cites pages rather than folios.

date_statements.label: KB nominal catalogue dating
date_statements.value: second half of the fourteenth century
date_statements.notes: This is the date printed on the older nominal catalogue card.

date_statements.label: Holthausen palaeographic caveat
date_statements.notes: Holthausen reports Stephens's fourteenth-century dating but says the script could equally belong to the fifteenth century.

holding.notes: The current public catalogue punctuation is X 90.

physical_description.support: Paper except for two parchment leaves
notes: Holthausen describes several scribal hands and changes of ink and paper.
The manuscript contains medical and cosmetic recipes, a Latin-English plant list, charms in several languages, and two longer English medical poems.
The public catalogue card also records a microfilm negative, MfH 428.
No public online manuscript facsimile was found in the KB Digitalt or Manuscripta shelfmark searches recorded below as of 2026-07-11.

entities.label: KB microfilm negative MfH 428
entities.description: A microfilm negative associated with manuscript X 90 in the KB nominal catalogue.
notes: The card confirms the identifier but does not provide online images, extent, image quality, or current reproduction terms.

entities.label: KB manuscript historically designated Miscellany VII
entities.description: The separate Stockholm manuscript from which Holthausen printed the three charms in Section II. Its current shelfmark and present survival status have not been confirmed in an authoritative current catalogue.

holding.notes: The historical designation is well supported, but the current shelfmark must be confirmed by KB. Do not substitute X 83; that shelfmark describes an unrelated Andreas Drossander manuscript.

notes: Holthausen describes a miscellany written by multiple hands, with empty and partly or wholly torn leaves.

Contents described by Holthausen include notes on the 1596 Essex expedition to Cadiz aboard The John & Francis, prayers attributed to Elizabeth I, religious and political material, a mercury recipe, and the three charms.
Holthausen places the recipe and charms near the opening of the manuscript but supplies no stable modern folio or page reference.
The manuscript formerly belonged to Christian Ravius, whose name Holthausen renders Christ. Ray.

entities.label: Stephens 1844 prior edition

bibliographic.citation: George Stephens, ‘Extracts in Prose and Verse from an Old English Medical Manuscript, Preserved in the Royal Library at Stockholm,’ Archaeologia 30 (1844): 349–418; separately issued, London: J. B. Nichols and Son, 1844.
bibliographic.title: Extracts in Prose and Verse from an Old English Medical Manuscript, Preserved in the Royal Library at Stockholm

notes: Holthausen cites a separate offprint whose internal pagination differs from the Archaeologia pagination.
The same three charms later printed in Holthausen Section II occur on separate-offprint pages 50–51, corresponding to Archaeologia pages 398–399.
Holthausen states that his starred Section I items 1, 18, 19, 20, 22, and 28 were not printed by Stephens.

entities.label: Stephens 1847 catalogue of British and French manuscripts at the Royal Library in Stockholm

bibliographic.citation: George Stephens, Förteckning öfver de förnämsta brittiska och fransyska handskrifterna uti Kongl. Bibliotheket i Stockholm (Stockholm: P. A. Norstedt & Söner, 1847).
bibliographic.title: Förteckning öfver de förnämsta brittiska och fransyska handskrifterna uti Kongl. Bibliotheket i Stockholm

notes: Historical Miscellany VII is described on printed page 25.
Medical Miscellany XIV, now X 90, is described on printed pages 40 and following.

entities.label: Local 14-TIFF scan set of Holthausen 1897
entities.description: A ZIP archive in the CME source history containing 14 black-and-white TIFF images of the printed 1897 article. It is not a manuscript facsimile.

physical_description.extent: 14 TIFF images; 3,147,835 uncompressed bytes reported by the ZIP directory
physical_description.format: One-bit black-and-white TIFF images in a ZIP archive
notes: The first image is 3296 by 5088 pixels and shows printed page 75 of Holthausen's article.
The archive filenames are 00000001.TIF through 00000014.TIF.

entities.reproduction_format: digital page images of a printed edition
notes: The archive's acquisition source, scanning institution, and original reuse terms have not yet been documented.

entities.label: Müller 1929 edition of X 90 prose recipes

bibliographic.citation: Gottfried Müller, Aus mittelenglischen Medizintexten: Die Prosarezepte des Stockholmer Miszellankodex X. 90, Kölner anglistische Arbeiten 10 (Leipzig: B. Tauchnitz, 1929).
bibliographic.title: Aus mittelenglischen Medizintexten: Die Prosarezepte des Stockholmer Miszellankodex X. 90

notes: This is a later, broader scholarly edition of prose recipes from X 90 and is useful for comparison; it is not an upstream source of CME00099.

scope.notes: The identifiers, title, structure, and content match, and the repository describes its source snapshot as drawn from CME. The exact transfer date and any intermediate normalization step are not documented.

object.description: Holthausen's complete article on printed pages 75–88

object.description: Selected passages identified by the manuscript's own page numbering
locators: page 85 (source text begins on page 80, but Holthausen omits the preceding portion)

object.description: The manuscript passages selected for Section I

scope.notes: Holthausen says he published the pieces after a new collation.

subject.description: Section II, items II.1–3 and associated notes

object.description: Three charms near the manuscript's opening, following a mercury recipe
locators: descriptive locator only; no verified current folio or page number

subject.description: Prefaces, item selection, and notes in Sections I and II

object.description: Stephens's earlier descriptions and editions of passages from both Stockholm manuscripts

scope.notes: This is a prior edition consulted by Holthausen. Because Holthausen states that he newly collated the manuscript and identifies items absent from Stephens, Stephens must not be treated as the sole intermediate source.

subject.description: Stephens's selections in prose and verse from the medical manuscript

object.description: Selected contents of Medical Miscellany XIV, now X 90

subject.description: The same three charms later printed by Holthausen

object.description: Three charms near the manuscript's opening
Coverage is not stated on the public catalogue card

subject.description: All 14 TIFF page images in CME00099.zip

subject.description: Müller's edition of the prose recipes

object.description: A broader set of X 90 prose recipes than Holthausen's 1897 selection

scope.notes: This later edition is a comparison resource, not an upstream source of CME00099.

rights.component: The CME00099 digital text and materials presented by University of Michigan
rights.basis: The item and collection pages expressly state that the materials are in the public domain and apply the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.
notes: No Copyright–United States is a rights statement, not an affirmative worldwide license.
The statement must not be generalized to a newly supplied KB facsimile.

rights.component: Upstream CME transcription and underlying 1897 source content represented in the local XML
rights.basis: The local artifact is supported as a version of U-M CME00099, which U-M marks No Copyright–United States; the immediate edition was published in 1897.
notes: This assessment does not override the repository's licenses for new project-authored code, metadata, formatting, or outputs.

rights.component: The 1897 edition, transcription, selection, and editorial notes
rights.basis: The work was published in 1897, well before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works; U-M independently marks the CME item based on it No Copyright–United States.

rights.component: Any selection, transcription choices, notes, or other editorial contribution that meets the applicable originality threshold
rights.basis: Holthausen died in 1956. Swedish Copyright Act section 43 gives the ordinary term through the end of the seventieth year after the author's death, yielding a cautious end date of 2026-12-31 for protectable contributions.
notes: This is a term calculation, not a conclusion that every transcription or editorial detail is sufficiently original to be protected.
On the ordinary calculation, any such term ends after 2026-12-31; re-review this record on or after 2027-01-01.
Other jurisdictions can apply different rules.

rights.component: The anonymous medieval textual content of the manuscript
rights.basis: The manuscript text dates from the fourteenth or fifteenth century and is far outside any U.S. copyright term.
notes: This statement concerns the old textual content, not modern photographs, scans, catalogue prose, transcriptions, or provider terms.

rights.basis: The manuscript is dated 1597 and its content is far outside any U.S. copyright term.
notes: This statement does not cover a modern reproduction or a modern scholarly edition.

rights.component: Any new digital photographs or scans supplied by KB in response to a reproduction order
rights.jurisdiction: Sweden and the intended place of reuse
rights.basis: No item-specific images, delivery terms, or written reuse determination are presently recorded. KB's general order page says it charges no usage or publication fee, but that is not an item-specific rights grant.
notes: Before committing supplied facsimiles, preserve KB's written response and determine whether attribution, source credit, delivery conditions, photographic rights, or other restrictions apply.
No manuscript facsimile is included in this repository as part of this record.

rights.component: The microfilm images and any scans made from them
rights.jurisdiction: Sweden and the intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The catalogue card identifies the microfilm but does not state its creation date, creator, image rights, or reproduction terms.

rights.component: The 1844 edition, transcription, and editorial text
rights.basis: The work was published in 1844, well before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: The Wikimedia Commons/Internet Archive PDF digital surrogate
rights.basis: The Commons file page marks the scan with the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark.
notes: The Public Domain Mark is a rights label, not a license.

rights.component: The Bavarian State Library digital page images and IIIF surrogate
rights.basis: The BSB digital object is labeled No Copyright–Non-Commercial Use Only 1.0.
notes: NoC-NC is not an affirmative open license. Link to the scan for reference and review the provider statement before redistributing its images, especially in a repository supporting commercial publication.

rights.component: The 14 TIFF image files as digital reproductions
rights.basis: The local archive has no identified scanning source or image-level rights statement in the provenance evidence reviewed for this manifest.
notes: The underlying 1897 printed work is public domain in the United States, but that alone does not document the provenance or provider terms of these specific files.

rights.basis: The work was published in 1929, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.
notes: This record does not determine the edition's status outside the United States or grant rights in HathiTrust's interface or metadata.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters printed in italics; represented in the repository XML by HI elements with REND=italic
editorial_practices.description: Holthausen expands manuscript contractions and uses italic type to identify the supplied letters. These italics generally encode editorial expansion, not emphasis or an omission in the manuscript.
editorial_practices.scope: Holthausen's transcribed manuscript text, especially Section I
notes: A manuscript facsimile is needed to determine the exact abbreviation mark, suspension, or contraction that underlies each italic expansion.

editorial_practices.representation: Bold type in the printed edition
editorial_practices.description: Holthausen represents manuscript text written in red with bold type rather than red ink.
editorial_practices.scope: Transcribed manuscript text where rubrication occurs

editorial_practices.description: Holthausen regularizes uppercase and lowercase usage according to contemporary editorial practice rather than preserving the manuscript's capitalization exactly.
Holthausen regularizes punctuation according to contemporary editorial practice rather than preserving manuscript punctuation exactly.
Holthausen states that he prepared the Section I selection after a new collation of the manuscript, while consulting Stephens's earlier edition.
Holthausen marks with an asterisk the Section I numbers not previously published by Stephens: 1, 18, 19, 20, 22, and 28.

evidence.citation: General Corpus README, ‘Intro.’
evidence.summary: The repository describes its working source snapshot as drawn from the Corpus of Middle English.

evidence.citation: CME00099 XML source in the pinned CME submodule.
evidence.locator: IDG, BIBNO, VID, and complete TEXT
evidence.summary: The local XML identifies itself as CME00099 and contains the complete title, prefaces, items, notes, and postscript corresponding to the U-M item and Holthausen article.

evidence.citation: Holthausen 1897 as encoded in CME00099, Section I preface.
evidence.summary: Holthausen identifies Medical Miscellany XIV, discusses its date, support, hands, contents, Stephens's prior edition, his new collation, and his expansion, rubrication, capitalization, and punctuation conventions.

evidence.citation: CME00099 Section I item heads and manuscript-page milestones.
evidence.summary: The item-level manuscript page markers support the X 90 page mapping. Asterisks identify items 1, 18, 19, 20, 22, and 28 as absent from Stephens.

evidence.citation: Holthausen 1897 as encoded in CME00099, Section II preface.
evidence.summary: Holthausen identifies a distinct 1597 English Miscellany VII, describes its contents and condition, and states that Stephens had printed the same three charms on offprint pages 50–51.

evidence.citation: Local CME00099.zip image archive in the pinned CME submodule.
evidence.summary: The archive contains 14 TIFF files. Visual inspection of the first image shows printed page 75 and the title/opening of Holthausen's 1897 article, establishing that the set images the printed edition rather than a manuscript.
notes: The ZIP directory reports filenames 00000001.TIF through 00000014.TIF and timestamps of 2000-05-18; timestamps are not treated as documented scan dates.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME00099 item information.
evidence.summary: The item page identifies Holthausen, Halle a. S.: Max Niemeyer, 1897; exposes the entire text; and marks the materials public domain with a DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection home and rights statement.
evidence.locator: Collection description and Rights and Permissions
evidence.summary: U-M says the collection's 2016–2018 batch was manually keyed from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading and basic TEI markup; it provides bulk XML and applies No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: Anglia 19 (1897), Internet Archive item angliazeitschrif19tbuoft, Holthausen article at printed pages 75–88.
evidence.locator: viewer leaf n89 through the article ending on printed page 88
evidence.summary: The full scan confirms the printed edition's bibliographic details, source statements, item scopes, and editorial conventions. Internet Archive metadata reports volume 19 and no visible copyright notice for the 1897 item.

evidence.citation: Kungliga biblioteket, manuscript nominal catalogue card 21655, X 90.
evidence.summary: The card identifies X 90 as Medical miscellanies, dates it to the latter half of the fourteenth century, and records ‘Även MfH 428 (neg.)’ for a microfilm negative.

evidence.citation: National Library of Sweden, ‘Order copies.’
evidence.locator: Order form, fees, and contact details
evidence.summary: KB provides an online reproduction-order form and info@kb.se. It says customers pay production, administration, and postage, while KB does not charge usage or publication fees.

evidence.citation: National Library of Sweden, ‘Find and request manuscripts and personal archives.’
evidence.locator: Search services, Special Reading Room requests, questions, and copy orders
evidence.summary: KB explains its manuscript catalogues, enquiry form, registered-reader access in the Special Reading Room, possible referral to microfilm or facsimile, and fee-based reproduction service.

evidence.citation: Exact public shelfmark searches for X 90 in KB Digitalt and Manuscripta.
evidence.summary: No indexed public facsimile result for shelfmark X 90 was returned by either service on the date checked.
notes: This is negative search evidence, not proof that no digitization or non-public preservation image exists.

evidence.citation: George Stephens, Extracts in Prose and Verse from an Old English Medical Manuscript, 1844 separate offprint, Internet Archive b22009620 and Wikimedia Commons.
evidence.summary: The scan and metadata confirm the title, author, 1844 London publication, relationship to Archaeologia 30 pages 349–418, and the contents used as a prior edition by Holthausen. Commons applies the Public Domain Mark to the file.

evidence.citation: George Stephens, Förteckning öfver de förnämsta brittiska och fransyska handskrifterna uti Kongl. Bibliotheket i Stockholm, 1847, BSB bsb10800271.
evidence.locator: printed page 25 for Historical Miscellany VII; printed page 40 and following for Medical Miscellany XIV
evidence.summary: The catalogue provides the historical manuscript designations and descriptions used by Holthausen. The BSB surrogate carries a No Copyright–Non-Commercial Use Only statement.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Biographie, ‘Holthausen, Ferdinand.’
evidence.summary: The authority record gives Holthausen's life dates as 9 September 1860 to 19 September 1956 and GND 116969520.

evidence.citation: Sweden, Lag (1960:729) om upphovsrätt till litterära och konstnärliga verk, section 43.
evidence.summary: Section 43 states the ordinary term through the end of the seventieth year after the author's death.

evidence.citation: Sweden, Lag (1960:729) om upphovsrätt till litterära och konstnärliga verk, section 49 a.
evidence.summary: Section 49 a establishes a related right for qualifying photographic images and a fifty-year term, subject to its statutory scope and exceptions. Item-specific application to a future KB facsimile has not been determined.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: Works first published before the current statutory cutoff
evidence.summary: The Copyright Office term chart supports the public-domain status in the United States of works published in 1844, 1897, and 1929 as of 2026.

evidence.citation: HathiTrust Digital Library, catalogue record 103191411, Aus Mittelenglischen Medizin-texten.
evidence.locator: Bibliographic description and Full view link
evidence.summary: The record identifies Gottfried Müller, Leipzig: B. Tauchnitz, 1929, 215 pages, and provides a full-view Cornell copy.

open_questions.question: What is the current KB shelfmark and survival status of the 1597 manuscript historically called Miscellany VII?
next_steps: Ask KB to reconcile Stephens 1847 page 25 and Holthausen Section II with the current catalogue.
When confirmed, record the current shelfmark, catalogue URI, survival status, access route, and any reproduction identifier.
notes: No authoritative public source checked for this record established a modern shelfmark.
X 83 is explicitly not used because the current X 83 catalogue card concerns an unrelated manuscript.

open_questions.question: Does a complete digital or preservation facsimile of X 90 exist outside the indexed public KB services?
next_steps: Record KB's response to the reproduction enquiry without committing private contact details.
Ask whether MfH 428 can be digitized or whether newer preservation photography exists.
notes: No public copy found is not synonymous with not digitized.

open_questions.question: What exact copyright, credit-line, contractual, and redistribution terms will KB apply to any commissioned X 90 facsimile?
next_steps: Retain KB's written response and delivery terms.
Before adding images to git, create a facsimile entity with checksum, extent, technical metadata, creation date, provider, and an evidence-backed rights record.
If public redistribution is not permitted, record only the request/purchase route and a non-sensitive citation to the private reproduction.
notes: The project's commercial print-on-demand purpose makes it especially important not to infer image reuse rights from the public-domain status of the manuscript text.

open_questions.question: What exact U-M XML release, download date, and transformation history produced the pinned local CME00099 XML blob?
next_steps: Inspect older CME repository history and any surviving U-M bulk-download release notes.
Add release identifiers and transformation steps if documentary evidence is found.

open_questions.question: Who created the local CME00099 TIFF scan set, when was it scanned, from which physical copy, and under what terms?
next_steps: Search surviving CME production records for the archive checksum, filenames, or scanning workflow.
Until provenance is documented, link to the archive but do not treat the image files as an openly licensed manuscript facsimile.

open_questions.question: What dating and codicological description does the current scholarly consensus assign to X 90?
next_steps: Consult a current manuscript catalogue or a modern codicological study and preserve the older KB card date as a separate historical assertion.
notes: Do not silently replace the old catalogue's latter-fourteenth-century date with a modern early-fifteenth-century date without citing the modern source.
